wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ResolveNet introduction and overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as a CNCF-grade Mega Agent platform that unifies Agent Skills, Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) Workflows, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) under a single intelligent routing layer. Document the architecture overview showing the relationship between clients, Higress AI gateway, platform services, and agent runtime. Describe the key benefits including LLM-powered meta-routing, Chinese LLM support, cloud-native deployment, and multi-modal interfaces. Include the technology stack overview with Go, Python, React/TypeScript, and AgentScope/Higress integration. Provide context about the project's goals, target audience, and use cases. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both beginners and experienced developers.

Detail the health check implementation using gRPC health service and reflection support for debugging. Document the HTTP server configuration, route registration, and middleware integration. Explain the server lifecycle management, error handling patterns, and logging integration. Include examples of server startup sequences, graceful termination, and operational monitoring. Address performance considerations, resource management, and deployment patterns.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Python runtime engine that orchestrates agent execution and manages the AgentScope integration. Explain the runtime server implementation for gRPC communication with platform services, including request handling, response formatting, and error propagation. Detail the context management system that maintains conversation history, execution metadata, and state persistence. Document the lifecycle hooks for initialization, execution phases, and cleanup processes. Include examples of runtime integration patterns, custom lifecycle extensions, and debugging techniques for runtime operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: FTA Fundamentals and Concepts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) fundamentals and core concepts. Explain the theoretical foundations of FTA including fault tree construction principles, event modeling, and probability calculations. Document the hierarchical structure of fault trees with root events, intermediate events, and basic events. Detail the concept of conditional dependencies and how faults propagate through the tree. Explain the difference between top events (system failures) and failure events (component failures). Include practical examples of common FTA scenarios such as system reliability analysis, safety assessment, and risk evaluation. Address the mathematical foundations including probability theory, Boolean algebra, and fault tree semantics. Provide guidance on when to use FTA versus other analytical methods.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ResolveNet: Unified Mega Agent Platform

## Project Introduction

ResolveNet is a CNCF-grade open-source Mega Agent platform designed to unify three core AI capabilities under a single intelligent routing layer. Built on AgentScope for agent orchestration and Higress for AI gateway capabilities, it serves as a comprehensive solution for enterprise-grade agent management and execution.

### Purpose Statement
ResolveNet addresses the complexity of modern AI agent systems by providing a unified platform that seamlessly integrates Agent Skills, Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) Workflows, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) capabilities through intelligent routing mechanisms.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Unified Intelligence**: Combine multiple AI capabilities (skills, workflows, RAG) under one intelligent selector
- **Enterprise-Grade**: Provide cloud-native deployment, observability, and production-ready infrastructure
- **Extensible Architecture**: Support plugin-based skills and configurable routing strategies
- **Multi-Modal Interface**: Offer CLI, TUI, and WebUI for different operational needs

### Target Audience
- Enterprise developers building AI agent systems
- DevOps teams requiring cloud-native deployment
- Platform engineers managing multi-agent workflows
- Organizations needing fault-tolerant AI solutions

## Technical Architecture

ResolveNet follows a microservices architecture with clear separation between platform services, agent runtime, and user interfaces:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "User Interfaces"
        CLI[CLI/TUI]
        WebUI[WebUI]
        API[External API]
    end
    
    subgraph "Gateway Layer"
        Gateway[Higress AI Gateway]
        Auth[Authentication]
        Router[Model Routing]
        Limits[Rate Limiting]
    end
    
    subgraph "Platform Services (Go)"
        APIServer[REST/gRPC API Server]
        Registries[Agent/Skill/Workflow Registries]
        EventBus[Event Bus]
        ConfigMgr[Configuration Management]
    end
    
    subgraph "Runtime Layer (Python)"
        Selector[Intelligent Selector]
        FTAEngine[FTA Engine]
        SkillExecutor[Skill Executor]
        RAGPipeline[RAG Pipeline]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        Postgres[(PostgreSQL)]
        Redis[(Redis)]
        NATS[(NATS)]
    end
    
    CLI --> Gateway
    WebUI --> Gateway
    API --> Gateway
    
    Gateway --> APIServer
    APIServer --> Postgres
    APIServer --> Redis
    APIServer --> NATS
    
    APIServer <---> Registries
    APIServer <---> ConfigMgr
    
    APIServer --> Selector
    Selector --> FTAEngine
    Selector --> SkillExecutor
    Selector --> RAGPipeline
    
    FTAEngine --> Postgres
    SkillExecutor --> Postgres
    RAGPipeline --> Postgres
```

### Component Breakdown

**Platform Services (Go)**: Core backend services handling API management, registries, and system orchestration
**Agent Runtime (Python)**: Intelligent agent execution engine with routing capabilities  
**User Interfaces**: Multiple access points including CLI, TUI, and WebUI
**Gateway Layer**: Higress-based AI gateway for authentication, routing, and rate limiting
**Data Layer**: PostgreSQL for persistent storage, Redis for caching, NATS for event streaming

### Design Patterns

The platform employs several key architectural patterns:

- **Microservices Pattern**: Clear separation between platform services and runtime components
- **Intelligent Routing Pattern**: LLM-powered meta-router for dynamic capability selection
- **Plugin Architecture**: Extensible skill system with sandboxed execution
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: NATS-based messaging for loose coupling
- **Registry Pattern**: Centralized management of agents, skills, and workflows

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Platform Server**: `cmd/resolvenet-server/main.go`
- Initializes configuration and server components
- Handles graceful shutdown and signal processing
- Provides REST/gRPC API endpoints

**CLI Application**: `cmd/resolvenet-cli/main.go`
- Uses Cobra framework for command-line interface
- Supports subcommands for agents, skills, workflows, and RAG operations
- Integrates with Viper for configuration management

**TUI Dashboard**: `internal/tui/app.go`
- Bubble Tea-based terminal user interface
- Interactive navigation between system views
- Real-time system monitoring capabilities

### Core Modules

**Intelligent Selector**: `python/src/resolvenet/selector/selector.py`
- Pluggable routing strategies (LLM, rule-based, hybrid)
- Three-stage processing: intent analysis, context enrichment, route decision
- Dynamic capability selection based on user input and system context

**FTA Engine**: `python/src/resolvenet/fta/engine.py`
- Fault Tree Analysis workflow execution
- Bottom-up evaluation of gates and events
- Support for AND, OR, and voting gates
- Asynchronous streaming of execution progress

**RAG Pipeline**: `python/src/resolvenet/rag/pipeline.py`
- End-to-end document processing pipeline
- Support for Milvus and Qdrant vector databases
- Cross-encoder reranking capabilities
- Configurable embedding models

**Execution Engine**: `python/src/resolvenet/runtime/engine.py`
- Orchestrates agent execution lifecycle
- Manages execution context and state
- Streams real-time execution progress

### Configuration Approach

**Centralized Configuration**: `configs/resolvenet.yaml`
- Hierarchical configuration with environment variable overrides
- Support for multiple configuration file locations
- Comprehensive settings for all system components

**Protocol Buffer Definitions**: `api/proto/resolvenet/v1/`
- gRPC service definitions for platform APIs
- Strongly-typed message schemas
- Cross-language compatibility

### External Dependencies

**Go Dependencies**:
- `github.com/spf13/cobra` - CLI framework
- `github.com/spf13/viper` - Configuration management  
- `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea` - TUI framework
- `google.golang.org/grpc` - gRPC communication

**Python Dependencies**:
- AgentScope for agent orchestration
- Pydantic for data validation
- Asyncio for concurrent execution
- Various LLM providers (OpenAI, Qwen, Wenxin, Zhipu)

**WebUI Dependencies**:
- React 18 with TypeScript
- TanStack React Query for data fetching
- React Flow for FTA visualization
- Zustand for state management

### Integration Points

**Protocol Buffer Communication**: gRPC-based service communication between platform and runtime
**Event Streaming**: NATS-based pub/sub for system events and notifications
**Database Integration**: PostgreSQL for persistent storage with connection pooling
**Container Orchestration**: Docker and Kubernetes support for deployment

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Gateway
    participant API
    participant Selector
    participant Runtime
    
    Client->>+Gateway: Request
    Gateway->>+API: Forward request
    API->>+Selector: Route decision
    Selector->>+Runtime: Execute task
    Runtime-->>-Selector: Results
    Selector-->>-API: Final response
    API-->>-Gateway: Response
    Gateway-->>-Client: Final response
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Intelligent Selector**: LLM-powered meta-router that dynamically routes requests to appropriate subsystems based on intent analysis and context enrichment.

**FTA Workflow Engine**: Comprehensive fault tree analysis with support for complex logical gates (AND, OR, voting) and leaf evaluators (skills, RAG, LLM).

**Agent Skill System**: Plugin-based architecture with sandboxed execution, manifest-based configuration, and community skill registry.

**RAG Pipeline**: Complete document processing pipeline supporting ingestion, vector indexing, semantic retrieval, and cross-encoder reranking.

**Multi-Modal Interfaces**: CLI, TUI, and WebUI provide different operational modes for various use cases.

### Implementation Highlights

**Cloud-Native Deployment**: `deploy/` directory provides Docker, Helm, and Kubernetes configurations for production deployment.

**Observability**: OpenTelemetry integration with metrics, tracing, and structured logging across all components.

**Development Tools**: Comprehensive Makefile with build, test, lint, and deployment automation.

**Testing Strategy**: Multi-layered testing including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> InputReceived
    InputReceived --> IntentAnalysis: User Input
    IntentAnalysis --> ContextEnrichment: Analyze Intent
    ContextEnrichment --> RouteDecision: Enrich Context
    RouteDecision --> FTAExecution: FTA Route
    RouteDecision --> SkillExecution: Skill Route  
    RouteDecision --> RAGExecution: RAG Route
    RouteDecision --> DirectExecution: Direct Route
    
    FTAExecution --> [*]: Complete
    SkillExecution --> [*]: Complete
    RAGExecution --> [*]: Complete
    DirectExecution --> [*]: Complete
```

The platform represents a sophisticated approach to AI agent orchestration, combining multiple complementary technologies into a cohesive system that can handle complex enterprise requirements while maintaining flexibility and extensibility.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [go.mod](go.mod)
- [cmd/resolvenet-server/main.go](cmd/resolvenet-server/main.go)
- [cmd/resolvenet-cli/main.go](cmd/resolvenet-cli/main.go)
- [python/src/resolvenet/runtime/engine.py](python/src/resolvenet/runtime/engine.py)
- [python/src/resolvenet/selector/selector.py](python/src/resolvenet/selector/selector.py)
- [python/src/resolvenet/fta/engine.py](python/src/resolvenet/fta/engine.py)
- [python/src/resolvenet/rag/pipeline.py](python/src/resolvenet/rag/pipeline.py)
- [web/package.json](web/package.json)
- [configs/resolvenet.yaml](configs/resolvenet.yaml)
- [internal/cli/root.go](internal/cli/root.go)
- [internal/tui/app.go](internal/tui/app.go)
- [api/proto/resolvenet/v1/agent.proto](api/proto/resolvenet/v1/agent.proto)
- [Makefile](Makefile)

</blog>
